name: TensorFlow
description: An open-source platform developed by Google for building and deploying machine learning and deep learning models at scale.
social_impact: Used in healthcare diagnostics, satellite imagery analysis, and real-time translation services across global platforms.
example_code:
import tensorflow as tf
model = tf.keras.Sequential([tf.keras.layers.Dense(10, activation='relu'), tf.keras.layers.Dense(1)])
model.compile(optimizer='adam', loss='mse')
print(model.summary())

name: PyTorch
description: An open-source deep learning framework with a dynamic computation graph, widely preferred for research and production model development.
social_impact: Used in drug discovery pipelines, medical imaging, and autonomous vehicle perception systems at major research institutions.
example_code:
import torch
x = torch.tensor([1.0, 2.0, 3.0])
linear = torch.nn.Linear(3, 1)
output = linear(x)
print(output)

name: Scikit-learn
description: A Python library providing simple and consistent APIs for classical machine learning algorithms including classification, regression, and clustering.
social_impact: Used in credit risk scoring, disease classification, and fraud detection across financial and healthcare sectors.
example_code:
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression
import numpy as np
X = np.array([[1], [2], [3]])
y = np.array([2, 4, 6])
model = LinearRegression().fit(X, y)
print(model.predict([[4]]))

name: Keras
description: A high-level neural networks API supporting multiple backends including TensorFlow, JAX, and PyTorch, designed for fast prototyping and production deployment.
dependencies: TensorFlow, JAX, or PyTorch (any one backend)
social_impact: Used in rapid prototyping of clinical AI models, environmental monitoring systems, and academic research due to its beginner-friendly API.
example_code:
from tensorflow import keras
model = keras.Sequential([keras.layers.Dense(8, activation='relu', input_shape=(4,)), keras.layers.Dense(1, activation='sigmoid')])
model.compile(optimizer='adam', loss='binary_crossentropy')
print(model.summary())

name: Gymnasium
description: The maintained successor to OpenAI Gym, providing a standard API and a collection of environments for developing and benchmarking reinforcement learning algorithms.
social_impact: Used in robotics simulation, autonomous control research, and academic RL coursework to provide reproducible benchmarking environments.
example_code:
import gymnasium as gym
env = gym.make('CartPole-v1')
obs, info = env.reset()
for _ in range(10):
    obs, reward, done, truncated, info = env.step(env.action_space.sample())
env.close()

name: XGBoost
description: A scalable and efficient gradient boosting library optimized for speed and performance, widely used in structured data competitions and production pipelines.
social_impact: Widely deployed in bank fraud detection, insurance risk modeling, and clinical outcome prediction.
example_code:
import xgboost as xgb
from sklearn.datasets import load_iris
X, y = load_iris(return_X_y=True)
model = xgb.XGBClassifier()
model.fit(X, y)
print(model.predict(X[:3]))

name: LightGBM
description: A fast, distributed gradient boosting framework using histogram-based algorithms for efficient training on large datasets.
social_impact: Used in large-scale recommendation engines, real-time bidding platforms, and patient outcome prediction in hospital systems.
example_code:
import lightgbm as lgb
from sklearn.datasets import load_iris
X, y = load_iris(return_X_y=True)
model = lgb.LGBMClassifier()
model.fit(X, y)
print(model.predict(X[:3]))

name: CatBoost
description: A gradient boosting library by Yandex that handles categorical features natively without manual encoding, reducing preprocessing effort.
social_impact: Used in e-commerce ranking, healthcare risk stratification, and financial credit scoring where datasets contain many categorical variables.
example_code:
from catboost import CatBoostClassifier
from sklearn.datasets import load_iris
X, y = load_iris(return_X_y=True)
model = CatBoostClassifier(verbose=0)
model.fit(X, y)
print(model.predict(X[:3]))

name: Dask-ML
description: A scalable machine learning library built on top of Dask, enabling parallel and distributed computation for datasets that exceed memory limits.
social_impact: Used in genomics pipelines, large-scale epidemiological studies, and climate science where datasets are too large for single-machine processing.
example_code:
import dask.array as da
from dask_ml.linear_model import LinearRegression
X = da.random.random((100, 4), chunks=50)
y = da.random.random(100, chunks=50)
model = LinearRegression()
model.fit(X, y)

name: MLflow
description: An open-source platform for managing the full machine learning lifecycle, including experiment tracking, model packaging, and deployment.
social_impact: Used by teams in regulated industries such as finance and healthcare to maintain reproducibility, audit trails, and compliance in ML deployments.
example_code:
import mlflow
with mlflow.start_run():
    mlflow.log_param('learning_rate', 0.01)
    mlflow.log_metric('accuracy', 0.95)
    print('Run logged successfully')

name: Hugging Face Transformers
description: A library providing thousands of pretrained transformer models for NLP, computer vision, and audio tasks, with support for PyTorch, TensorFlow, and JAX.
social_impact: Used in clinical note summarization, multilingual chatbots, and automated legal document analysis across the public and private sectors.
example_code:
from transformers import pipeline
classifier = pipeline('sentiment-analysis')
result = classifier('I love building AI projects!')
print(result)

name: DeepSpeed
description: A deep learning optimization library from Microsoft that enables efficient training of very large models using techniques like ZeRO memory optimization and pipeline parallelism.
social_impact: Enables smaller organizations to train large language models by significantly reducing GPU memory requirements and training time.
example_code:
import deepspeed
import torch
model = torch.nn.Linear(10, 1)
engine, _, _, _ = deepspeed.initialize(model=model, config='ds_config.json')
print('DeepSpeed engine initialized')

name: JAX
description: A library by Google for high-performance numerical computing with automatic differentiation and XLA compilation, used in advanced ML research.
social_impact: Used in protein structure prediction research (AlphaFold), climate simulation, and large-scale physics modeling in scientific computing.
example_code:
import jax.numpy as jnp
from jax import grad
f = lambda x: x ** 2 + 3 * x
df = grad(f)
print(df(2.0))

name: ONNX
description: An open format for representing machine learning models, enabling seamless transfer of models between different frameworks and deployment environments.
social_impact: Enables AI model portability in healthcare devices, edge computing, and cross-platform enterprise software without vendor lock-in.
example_code:
import onnx
model = onnx.load('model.onnx')
onnx.checker.check_model(model)
print('ONNX model is valid')

name: Apache MXNet
description: A scalable deep learning framework supporting multiple programming languages. Note: Apache MXNet was retired by the Apache Software Foundation in September 2023 and is no longer actively maintained.
social_impact: Was used in large-scale e-commerce personalization and content moderation at platforms like Amazon before its retirement.
example_code:
import mxnet as mx
from mxnet import nd
x = nd.array([[1, 2], [3, 4]])
print(x)
print(x.shape)

name: spaCy
description: An industrial-strength NLP library for Python offering fast and accurate pipelines for tokenization, named entity recognition, dependency parsing, and more.
social_impact: Used in legal document parsing, electronic health record extraction, and automated journalism to process large volumes of unstructured text.
example_code:
import spacy
nlp = spacy.load('en_core_web_sm')
doc = nlp('Apple is looking at buying a startup.')
for ent in doc.ents:
    print(ent.text, ent.label_)

name: NLTK
description: The Natural Language Toolkit, a comprehensive Python platform for working with human language data, widely used in academic NLP education and research.
social_impact: Used in public health surveillance systems that analyze social media to detect disease outbreaks, and in educational tools for automated language assessment.
example_code:
import nltk
nltk.download('punkt', quiet=True)
from nltk.tokenize import word_tokenize
text = 'Hello, welcome to SLIBAI!'
tokens = word_tokenize(text)
print(tokens)

name: Stanza
description: A Python NLP toolkit developed by Stanford supporting over 70 languages, providing accurate linguistic analysis including tokenization, POS tagging, and named entity recognition.
social_impact: Supports multilingual NLP for low-resource languages, used in cross-lingual humanitarian information retrieval and academic linguistic research.
example_code:
import stanza
stanza.download('en')
nlp = stanza.Pipeline('en')
doc = nlp('Stanford NLP is powerful.')
for sent in doc.sentences:
    print([word.text for word in sent.words])

name: TextBlob
description: A simple Python library for processing textual data, offering an easy-to-use API for common NLP tasks including sentiment analysis, POS tagging, and noun phrase extraction.
social_impact: Used in social media monitoring for mental health research and product feedback analysis, enabling rapid sentiment-based decision making.
example_code:
from textblob import TextBlob
blob = TextBlob('SLIBAI is an amazing project!')
print(blob.sentiment)
print(blob.tags)

name: AllenNLP
description: A PyTorch-based NLP research library built by AI2, designed for building and evaluating deep learning models for language tasks. Note: AllenNLP was deprecated by the Allen Institute for AI in June 2022 and is no longer maintained.
social_impact: Advanced NLP research for scientific literature analysis and clinical note processing before its deprecation in 2022.
example_code:
from allennlp.predictors.predictor import Predictor
predictor = Predictor.from_path('https://storage.googleapis.com/allennlp-public-models/bert-base-srl-2020.11.19.tar.gz')
result = predictor.predict(sentence='SLIBAI helps developers find AI tools.')
print(result['verbs'])
